fear: That his private meetings with a Loyalist contact — conducted to gather counter-intelligence — will be mistaken for treachery.
name: Dr. Joseph Warren
role: Physician, patriot leader, and intelligence coordinator for the Sons of Liberty
voice: Precise and clinical, with occasional flashes of passionate rhetoric — he chooses words the way he chooses instruments, deliberately and without waste.
secrets: He has been meeting privately with a Loyalist merchant to gather information about Crown troop movements — these meetings look deeply suspicious out of context.
He possesses a partial cipher key that can decode the letter in Dillworth's counting house, but he has been reluctant to share it because it would reveal his own covert activities.
knowledge: He knows that someone has been copying documents from the inner circle's correspondence for at least six weeks — the handwriting on certain intercepted notes does not match any known member.
He has a partial cipher key recovered from a Crown messenger three weeks ago — it matches the encoding style of the letter hidden in Dillworth's counting house.
publicFace: Calm, intelligent, and authoritative — the most composed man in any room; his medical training gives him an air of detached analytical precision.
trustLogic: He opens up to players who demonstrate analytical thinking and bring him specific, verifiable details; he stonewalls anyone who seems to be fishing or acting on emotion.
introAnchor: a Sons of Liberty physician and intelligence coordinator whose medical calm masks the most analytical mind in the room
privateGoal: Protect the Sons of Liberty's intelligence network at all costs — some of his sources are more compromising than tonight's action.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Deflects with a question: 'What precisely leads you to that conclusion? Walk me through your reasoning.'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never voluntarily reveal the name of his Loyalist intelligence contact — doing so would endanger that man's life.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes very still and very quiet: 'I would advise you to consider carefully what you are implying and whether you can support it.'
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Provides a full, methodical account of his counter-intelligence work and offers the cipher key to help decode the letter.
